page: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz, this instance includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

The server features 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, making it a high-density storage platform. It does not include any graphics processing units (GPUs). For external connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 56 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the i7i.metal-24xl demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, with Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations scoring in the top 10%. Multi-threaded decompression and Geekbench multi-core metrics also rank in the top 10%. For machine learning workloads, the server achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds for medium (7B) LLM models, though text generation speeds for large (70B) models drop to average levels, highlighting a performance tradeoff when executing large-scale generative tasks without dedicated GPU acceleration.

Given its bare-metal architecture, massive local NVMe storage capacity, and high memory density, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring direct hardware access and high I/O throughput without virtualization overhead. It is designed for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server configured with 96 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8559C processors and 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network speed of 56 Gbps, with no integrated GPUs. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded decompression. While it delivers strong prompt processing for medium LLM models, text generation for large models remains average, representing a key tradeoff of CPU-only execution. This server is structured for high-throughput database systems, software compilation, and data-intensive enterprise workloads. The bare-metal design eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the utility of the massive local storage and high-speed memory.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-threaded performance.

tagline: A bare-metal, storage-optimized platform combining massive local NVMe capacity with high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive database and data-processing workloads.

768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput data access
